content: When extracting a behaviour blob from entrypoint.sh into a new factory_core module, use this IO-injection discriminator: local filesystem reads/writes (transcript tails, artifact .md reads, JSONL appends, run-dir globbing) move INTO the Python module and are exercised directly in tests (the run_record.py precedent — _parse_archon_cost/cmd_assemble read local paths in-module); only genuinely external calls (gh api/gh issue, LLM subprocess calls like 'claude -p') stay bash-side or behind an injected io object (the epic_autopilot.py run_once(cfg, io, ...) precedent). Don't default to wrapping every external-ish call behind an io class — epic_autopilot's io abstraction earns its keep because run_once orchestrates several sequential external calls with branching between them; a single LLM-call-then-comment-post shape (e.g. run_post_mortem) doesn't need one, just pass the external call's string result into the pure Python function as a plain argument.

This repo has no fixtures/ or golden/ directory anywhere — golden-style tests hand-author inline dicts/json.dumps directly in the test file (see tests/test_run_record.py). When a refactor needs byte-compatibility with existing bash rendering logic and there's no docker/gh access to capture output by executing the old code, derive the expected-output literal by re-transcribing the bash/jq logic line-by-line into the test file ('golden by construction'), rather than introducing a new external fixture-file convention.
